items.title: Pete Hegseth Is Pushing Defense Employees to Volunteer With DHS
items.summary: The defense secretary has made clear that Pentagon managers are to encourage workers, including civilians, to volunteer to assist in the administration’s immigration crackdown.

items.title: Anker’s Discounted Power Station Can Power Your Next Camping Trip

items.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/Atlas Has Another Surprise: It’s Full of Alcohol
items.summary: Analysis of the tail of 3I/Atlas reveals that it contains an anomalous proportion of methanol, a class of alcohol used in fuels.

items.title: DOGE employee stole Social Security data and put it on a thumb drive, report says
items.summary: A whistleblower is accusing a former DOGE member of stealing a large number of Americans’ personal data while he was working at the Social Security Administration, with the plan of using it at his new job.

items.summary: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

It is the same ideology, the same nuclear ambitions, the same…

items.title: Cosmic voids look empty but they may be tearing the universe apart
items.summary: Cosmic voids may seem like the emptiest places in the universe, stripped of matter, radiation, and even dark matter. But they’re far from nothing. Even in these vast empty regions, the fundamental quantum fields that fill all of space remain, carrying a small but real amount of energy known as vacuum energy, or dark energy. While this energy is overwhelmed by matter in galaxies and clusters, in the deep emptiness of cosmic voids it becomes dominant.

items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

items.title: Scientists may have discovered a brand-new mineral on Mars
items.summary: Scientists studying Mars may have uncovered a brand-new mineral hidden in the planet’s ancient sulfate deposits. By combining laboratory experiments with orbital data, researchers identified an unusual iron sulfate—ferric hydroxysulfate—forming in layered deposits near the massive Valles Marineris canyon system. The mineral likely formed when sulfate-rich deposits left behind by ancient water were later heated by volcanic or geothermal activity, transforming their chemistry.

items.title: ‘Smart underwear’ measures how often humans fart
items.summary: “Zen digesters” rarely fart. “Hydrogen hyperproducers” fart a lot. Scientists are investigating what is typical.

items.title: Tree tops sparkle with electricity during thunderstorms
items.summary: Ultraviolet cameras captured faint electrical flashes from leaves and branches as storm charges built up in the atmosphere.

items.title: When the pressure’s off, this superconductor appears to break records
items.summary: A sudden release of pressure allowed a copper-based compound to superconduct at the highest temperature yet for atmospheric pressure, a study claims.
